items.title: Philippine Marcos orders crisis committee amid Mideast conflict
items.summary: Castro said the crisis body will focus on securing supply chains and ensuring the continuous availability of fuel and other basic commodities. The Palace official also clarified that while global tensions have driven up oil prices, the Philippines is not experiencing an oil supply crisis. "At present, this is not considered an oil crisis because we have complete and sufficient oil supply," Castro said, citing the Department of Energy (DOE).

items.title: Yeo Bee Yin announces arrival of identical twin boys
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Puchong member of Parliament Yeo Bee Yin and her husband, Lee Yeow Seng, have celebrated the arrival of identical twin boys, bringing their total number of sons to five. The twins, who made their "grand entrance" at 35 weeks on March 14, are Monochorionic Diamniotic (MCDA) twins, which means they shared a single placenta but developed in separate amniotic sacs. Despite the complexities typically associated with MCDA pregnancies, the twins were born healthy and without any major complications.

items.title: Rising voters in Tawau spark plan to to create second parliamentary seat
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 23 — Tawau could be divided into two parliamentary constituencies following a sharp increase in the number of registered voters in the area, according to Datuk Nizam Abu Bakar Titingan. Sabah Youth, Sports and Creative Economy Development Minister said the current Tawau parliamentary seat has more than 80,000 voters, highlighting the need for better representation, The Star reported today. The Tawau federal seat also covers three state constituencies: Apas, Balung and Sri Tanjong.

items.title: 'Petronas must be last resort for subsidies, not first response'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Calls to tap Petronas' profits to cushion surging fuel prices should be treated with extreme caution, with an expert warning the national oil company must remain a "last resort, not the first response" to market volatility. He contended that higher crude and LNG prices would naturally boost Petronas' earnings, providing a ready-made buffer to offset domestic price hikes. However, oil and gas analyst Jamil Ghani cautioned that this "short-term fix" ignores sustainable policy options and the growing operational pressures on Petronas.

items.title: Foreigners scramble as Immigration raids Selangor hotspots
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Foreigners scrambled to escape as Selangor Immigration Department officers arrived in multi-purpose vehicles (MPVs) during raids at two locations popular among migrants today. The raids under "Op Omnipresent", began at 11am and targeted the Taman Sri Muda morning market and the Section 15 commercial area. Selangor Immigration director Khairrul Aminus Kamaruddin said two special teams were deployed to conduct continuous monitoring and periodic inspections at identified hotspots throughout the Hari Raya Aidilfitri period.

items.title: Myanmar introduces work-from-home Wednesdays to conserve fuel
items.summary: YANGON: Myanmar introduced work-from-home on Wednesdays for government departments starting March 25, 2026, until further notice to help reduce fuel consumption, according to a statement published in the state-owned daily The Mirror on Monday, reported Xinhua. In response to potential fuel shortages linked to the ongoing conflict in West Asia, the government had previously implemented odd-even vehicle rotation systems and fuel rationing. Additionally, the government is working to secure fuel imports through various channels to ensure continued supply, it added.

items.title: Three detained to assist probe into fireworks stall blaze
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: Police have detained three caretakers of a fireworks stall in Taman Pekaka here, which was destroyed in a fire early yesterday. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the three were detained yesterday. "We have detained three caretakers of the said fireworks stall yesterday.

items.title: Japan's Takaichi vows all diplomatic efforts to ease West Asia tensions
items.summary: TOKYO: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said on Monday Japan will make "every possible diplomatic effort" in concert with the international community to ease tensions around the Strait of Hormuz amid escalating threats in the US-Israeli war with Iran, Kyodo News reported. Her comments come as financial markets, including in Japan, have been roiled by US President Donald Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to Iran over the weekend to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a key route for crude oil imports from the Middle East to Japan, or face attacks on energy infrastructure. "We are continuing to work with the international community and will make every possible diplomatic effort," Takaichi, who returned to Japan on Saturday from a summit with Trump in Washington, said in parliament.

items.title: Global energy watchdog says over 40 West Asia energy sites heavily damaged by conflict
items.summary: ANKARA: More than 40 energy assets across nine countries in West Asia have been "severely or very severely" damaged amid the ongoing conflict, the International Energy Agency said, according to media reports Sunday, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported. IEA Executive Director Fatih Birol said the damage could prolong disruptions to global supply chains even after the conflict ends. Birol said more than three weeks of fighting have disrupted the entire energy supply chain and nearly halted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: #NSTviral: Fireworks fired from moving 4WD on public road spark outrage [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 4WD vehicle seen firing fireworks while moving along a public road has sparked public outrage, with calls for police action over safety risks to motorists and pedestrians. The incident, uploaded on the Penduduk Alor Setar Facebook page, shows a vehicle carrying multiple fireworks being ignited one after another as it travels along a public road past other road users. Eyewitnesses said the fireworks were launched without apparent control, with sparks and bursts lighting up the sky above the busy road.

items.title: 11mil barrels per day supply hit by West Asia conflict – IEA
items.summary: JAKARTA: The world risks facing the most serious energy crisis in decades amid the conflict in West Asia, with the market losing 11 million barrels of oil per day, the head of the International Energy Agency (IEA), Fatih Birol, said on Monday, reported Sputnik/RIA Novosti. Both of them together 10 million barrels per day ... And today, only as of today, we lost 11 million barrels per day, so more than two major oil shocks put together," Birol said at the National Press Club of Australia. According to the IEA's forecast, global oil demand in 2026 will reach 104.77 million barrels per day, while OPEC expects consumption to reach 106.53 million barrels per day.

items.title: Zaid questions timing of Immigration raids during festive period
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former law minister Datuk Zaid Ibrahim has criticised the Selangor Immigration Department for conducting enforcement raids during the Hari Raya festive period, questioning the sensitivity of the timing. The criticism followed the implementation of "Ops Omnipresent", a statewide operation in which enforcement officers screened hundreds of individuals at high-traffic locations, including bus terminals and shopping centres, to verify residency status and travel documents. Selangor Immigration Department director Khairrul Aminus Kamaruddin said that the latest "Ops Omnipresent" operation across several hotspots in the Petaling district saw 137 individuals screened on the day, with no arrests recorded.

items.title: Price hikes, outlook cuts - What airlines are doing as fuel costs surge
items.summary: AIR FRANCE-KLM The French airline said on March 12 it planned to increase long-haul ticket prices to address surging jet fuel costs. It said cabin fares would rise by 50 euros (US$57) per round trip. AMERICAN AIRLINES The US carrier said on March 17 it expected a US$400 million increase in first-quarter expenses as jet fuel prices surge.

items.title: As temperatures soar, Kedah and Perlis worry about drying rivers and shrinking reservoirs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Residents in northern peninsular Malaysia are facing severe water shortages and rising temperatures as a prolonged heatwave continues to grip Kedah and Perlis. In Kampung Lubuk Tualang, Sik, Sungai Tebal is running unusually shallow, exposing sandbanks in several areas, while local hill water sources have dried up, forcing villagers to ration their limited supplies, Harian Metro reported today. The extreme heat is also affecting agriculture, with rice fields outside the Muda Agricultural Development Authority (Mada) areas reporting poor yields due to limited irrigation.

items.title: Decades-old Raya gathering unites four generations in Kepala Batas
items.summary: The celebration brought together four generations in a show of unity and enduring family ties. The gathering, held at the family's ancestral home in Bertam, drew relatives ranging from the eldest, Abdullah Talib, 82, to the youngest — a one-month-old baby — highlighting the family's lasting legacy. Despite around 140 members being unable to attend, the turnout reflected strong bonds within the family, which now spans four generations.

items.title: Gold, Silver slide sharply as rate cut hopes fade
items.summary: Spot gold fell about four per cent in early trading to just above US$4,300 per ounce, down more than 18 per cent since the start of US-Israeli attacks on Iran. Prices had initially surged to nearly US$5,420 in the early days of the conflict. Gold has fallen nearly US$1,300, or about 23 per cent, from its record high of close to US$5,600 in late January.

items.title: France ex-PM Jospin dies aged 88: Family
items.summary: PARIS: Former French prime minister Lionel Jospin, a Socialist who introduced the 35-hour work week and civil partnerships for gay couples, has died aged 88, his family said Monday. Jospin – who was head of government from 1997 to 2002 before losing to the far right in presidential polls – died on Sunday, they told AFP. Jospin ran for president in 2002, casting himself as a clean pair of hands compared with his conservative rival, the corruption-tainted but chummy and charismatic Jacques Chirac.

items.title: Selangor Immigration inspects 137 in festive sweep, no arrests made
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Selangor Immigration Department inspected 137 individuals during a festive-season enforcement operation dubbed "Ops Omnipresent" across several hotspots in the Petaling district today. The operation, which began at 10am, involved six officers and was led by Immigration Department deputy assistant director Gurpal Singh Manjit Singh. Enforcement teams focused on high-traffic locations, including Pasar Moden Taman Sri Muda and Dataran Otomobil in Seksyen 15 here.

items.title: Malaysia, Philippines, Vietnam among Asean's most exposed to West Asia oil supply risks
items.summary: In a research note, Maybank Investment Bank (Maybank IB) said among Asean countries, the Philippines' crude oil import exposure stands at 95 per cent, followed by Vietnam (88 per cent), Malaysia (69 per cent), Thailand (59 per cent), and Singapore (52 per cent). It said, however, that Indonesia has a more diversified import profile, with 20 per cent of its crude sourced from Gulf countries. Indonesia follows at 8.4 per cent, while Thailand stands at 6.8 per cent.

items.title: Vegetable prices set to rise as production costs soar, say farmers association
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Vegetable prices are expected to rise gradually in the near future following a spike in production costs. Cameron Highlands Malay Farmers Association chairman Datuk Syed Abd Rahman Syed Abd Rashid said the price hike is anticipated within the next week or two. "Consumers have the power to influence prices.

items.title: 3 Malaysians among 49 detained in Cambodia cyber scam raid
items.summary: PHNOM PENH: Three Malaysians were among 49 people arrested here during a raid on an alleged online scam operation, Cambodian authorities said, as they step up cybercrime crackdowns. A joint task force under the Phnom Penh Unified Command Committee carried out the operation today at a hotel in Khan Sen Sok, acting on orders from Phnom Penh Governor Khuong Sreng. The others arrested were 43 Chinese nationals, one Vietnamese woman, one Myanmar woman and one Cambodian woman.

items.title: Kedah police checking hospitals after viral claims of ‘mercun bola’ injuries in Pendang
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 23 — Police are obtaining further information from the hospital regarding claims that several individuals were injured after being hit by a ‘mercun bola’ (ball firecracker) in Tobiar, Pendang. Police will visit the hospital today to obtain full details regarding the incident. Earlier, the incident went viral on Facebook, showing pictures of several injured individuals with the caption: ‘tragedi pakar bom dari Tobiar, Pendang, Kedah..ujikaji custom mercun bola, tujuh mangsa beraya di hospital’ (‘Tragedy involving bomb expert from Tobiar, Pendang, Kedah… custom ball firecracker experiment, seven victims celebrating Aidilfitri in hospital).

items.title: Zahid extends birthday greetings to Sultan Ibrahim
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has extended his congratulations on the official birthday of the Sultan of Johor, Sultan Ibrahim, at today. In a Facebook post, Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, conveyed the greetings on behalf of his family, staff of the Deputy Prime Minister's Office and the Rural and Regional Development Ministry, as well as the leadership and members of Umno. "Honouring His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the Sultan of Johor Darul Ta'zim and Yang di-Pertuan Agong.

items.title: MACC proposes three new laws to curb public fund misappropriation, 'grand corruption'
items.summary: The first proposed law is an act addressing misconduct in public office, aimed at preventing improper behaviour among government officials that could lead to serious criminal offences. "The introduction of a law such as the Public Office Misconduct Act would make various forms of misappropriation or negligence by government personnel involving public funds a clear criminal offence. Elaborating on the proposed Welfare Act, he said a Welfare Commission should also be established to prevent misuse of donation funds or NGO funds sourced from public and corporate contributions.

items.title: Aidilfitri: 7,000 Malaysians, Thais cross at Rantau Panjang ICQS
items.summary: KOTA BARU: About 7,000 Malaysians and Thais have been recorded using the Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex in Rantau Panjang, Pasir Mas during the Aidilfitri festive period. Rantau Panjang Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) deputy commander Bakri Sulaiman said the number is expected to rise over the next few days as the school holidays are still ongoing. He said the cross-border movement was driven by Malaysians and Thais taking the opportunity to visit relatives in Kelantan and southern Thailand.

items.title: Mideast crisis puts Asia's earnings boom to the test
items.summary: Since late October, upgrades to earnings estimates have boosted Asian stock prices even as forward PE multiples have fallen. Since then, the index has risen 6.4 per cent while the forward PE has declined 11 per cent, indicating a 17 per cent upgrade to 12-month forward earnings per share (EPS) in about five and a half months. South Korea's earnings upswing was the starkest.

items.title: Suspected cable thieves flee Selangor suburb after resident confrontation [WATCH]
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA: Suspected cable thieves armed with a heavy wire cutter raided a Selangor suburb in daylight, fleeing with a severed cable after a resident shouted "cable thieves". The footage captures a resident confronting them and shouting "cable thieves", while one suspect remains in the vehicle clutching a severed cable as the group makes their escape. Stealing cables ... stealing cables ah," a resident was heard shouting as he read out the number plates of the two vehicles.

items.title: #NSTviral: Viral video of RTD vehicle overtaking on double line sparks outrage
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A viral video showing a Road Transport Department (RTD) vehicle overtaking on a double line sparked public outrage, with netizens questioning the apparent lack of enforcement. The clip drew sharp criticism, with many pointing to perceived double standards involving enforcement officers. "JPJ itself did not follow the law by overtaking on a double line.

items.title: Bukit Mertajam Hospital dismisses claims of relocation to avoid disrupting temple
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, March 23 — Bukit Mertajam Hospital’s Board of Visitors today dismissed allegations that the hospital would be relocated to avoid disrupting a temple. Board spokesperson Ng Yi Cheah, who lodged a police report today over the false claims, said the allegations appeared on social media on March 22. “The post titled ‘Hospital Bukit Mertajam akan dipindahkan agar tak ganggu kuil - Steven Sim Chee Keong’ was false, baseless, misleading and malicious in intent,” he said in a statement after filing the report at the Seberang Perai Tengah police headquarters.

items.title: BOJ's narrative shift signals dogged commitment to rate hikes
items.summary: TOKYO: The Bank of Japan is laying the groundwork for tweaks to its policy language in April, keeping alive the chance of a near-term interest rate hike as the weak yen and Middle East conflict pile inflationary pressures on the economy. Any such change would leave scope for the BOJ to hike rates, even if the board cuts its growth forecasts in new quarterly forecasts due at the April 27-28 policy meeting, analysts say. The new price gauge adds to data the BOJ looks at in determining Japan's underlying inflation, or price moves driven by domestic demand rather than cost-pushing factors.

items.title: Singapore core inflation at 1.4pct year-on-year in February, slightly higher than expected
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Singapore's key consumer price gauge rose 1.4 per cent in February from a year earlier, official data showed on Monday. The core inflation rate, which excludes private road transport and accommodation costs, was slightly higher than the median forecast of 1.3 per cent in a Reuters poll of economists. Headline inflation was 1.2 per cent in annual terms in February, slightly lower than the poll forecast of 1.3 per cent.

items.title: [UPDATED] Pilot, co-pilot killed in runway collision at New York airport
items.summary: NEW YORK: A plane carrying dozens of people collided with a fire truck late Sunday on a runway at New York's LaGuardia airport, killing the pilot and co-pilot and forcing a halt to flights, authorities and US media reported. The aircraft operated by Jazz Aviation, a regional partner of Air Canada, struck a firefighting truck on Runway 4 about 11.40pm on Sunday (0340 GMT Monday) as the vehicle drove to a separate incident, New York's port authority said. A preliminary passenger list showed 76 people on board, including four crew members, Jazz Aviation said in a statement.

items.title: Thailand must submit comments to US on Section 301 probe by April 15
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand must submit written comments to the United States on the US Section 301 trade investigation by April 15, the commerce ministry said on Monday. * Thailand is among 16 countries the US said would be subject to investigation under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974. * The country remains in discussions with the US following the original joint statement on tariff and non-tariff issues, she said.

items.title: Japan 'not thinking about' discussing Hormuz passage with Iran
items.summary: TOKYO: Tokyo is "not thinking about" calling on Iran to let Japanese tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, the foreign minister has said, after Tehran said it was ready to help. Asked about whether the government would urge Iran to let Japanese ships transit Hormuz, Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi said Sunday on a Fuji Television programme that "for the time being, we are not thinking about that." His comment came after Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi said in a telephone interview with Kyodo News Friday that Tehran was prepared to ensure safe passage for Japan.

items.title: Bangladeshi man drowns after falling into river in Perak
items.summary: IPOH, March 23 — A Bangladeshi man was found drowned after falling into the river near Kledang Timur 4, Taman Bandar Baru Menglembu, here, earlier this morning. Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the 29-year-old’s body was recovered after the department received an emergency call from the public at 8.35 am. He said 10 personnel and two fire engines from the Pasir Puteh Fire and Rescue Station were deployed to the scene.

items.title: Johor Immigration cites extensive borders, evolving syndicate tactics as key challenges
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 23 — Johor’s geographical challenges, with its extensive land and maritime borders, as well as the increasingly deceptive tactics used by illegal immigrant syndicates, which frequently change their modus operandi, are among the main challenges faced by the state Immigration Department in curbing illegal activities. State Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said these factors have led to the existence of numerous ‘rat trails’ (illegal routes) along the coastline, with areas such as Kota Tinggi, Mersing and Pontian becoming primary targets for syndicates smuggling illegal immigrants from Indonesia. Declining to reveal the exact hotspot locations, he said that the syndicates take advantage of illegal sea routes, using small boats at night to bring in illegal immigrants, especially from Indonesia.

items.title: Health Ministry: Malaysia’s medicine supply stable despite West Asia conflict
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 23 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has confirmed that the supply of medicines in the country is currently stable and under control despite the conflict in West Asia. In a statement today, MOH said that the short-term impact of the West Asia conflict is minimal based on current assessments. “The stock level of medicines at MOH facilities is maintained at between one and three months based on usage requirements, and is supported by additional buffer stock of up to two months at the concession company level for medicines supplied through the logistics concession,” it said.

items.title: After 40 years in the dark, Marudi village lights up just in time for Raya
items.summary: Deputy Minister in the Sarawak Premier’s Department (Labour, Immigration and Project Monitoring) Datuk Gerawat Gala said the electricity connection to the village was completed two weeks ago, just in time for the Raya celebration. “Kampung Seberang Lubok Nibong holds a special place in my heart as it is the only Muslim village in DUN N.78 Mulu,” he said, adding that the village has about 60 residents. Also present was the village headman Madlie Moss.

items.title: Future of Kapit seat: Nanta signals Alvin Runggah as next parliamentary candidate
items.summary: KAPIT, March 23 — Kapit MP Dato Sri Alexander Nanta Linggi has signalled that Datuk Alvin Runggah Jimbun is the person he intends to entrust with the Kapit parliamentary seat if he no longer contests in future elections. “If the time comes when I no longer contest as a parliamentary candidate for P215 Kapit, Datuk Alvin Runggah Jimbun will be the individual who will take over that responsibility. “Therefore, he needs the full support of the Kapit community to ensure that development continuity can be carried forward successfully,” he said during a Thanksgiving Dinner here on Sunday.

items.title: From 82 to 99? Sarawak assembly approves 17 new state seats, but Election Commission has final say
items.summary: KUCHING, March 23 — The Sarawak Legislative Assembly (DUN) has approved an increase in the number of state seats, expanding from 82 to 99 following the addition of 17 new constituencies. “At this stage, there is no report yet from SPR on whether the 17 new constituencies have been confirmed. However, increasing parliamentary seats would require a two-thirds majority in Parliament, which may be more challenging for the ruling government.

items.title: Soldier killed in crash involving two motorcycles and car in Alor Gajah
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, March 23 — A soldier was killed in a crash involving two motorcycles and a car along Jalan Padang Kemunting, Masjid Tanah here, early this morning. Melaka Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) chief Supt Muhammad Zaki Ramat said the crash also involved a SYM VF3i motorcycle and a Proton Saga car. “He then collided with a motorcycle and a car travelling in the opposite direction, from Pengkalan Balak towards Sungai Udang.

items.title: NRES strengthens weather monitoring with AI, big data to tackle climate change
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 23 — The Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES), through the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), is strengthening weather monitoring and forecasting capabilities to ensure the country remains prepared for climate change. Its minister, Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup, said the effort includes integrating artificial intelligence (AI) and big data analytics into the Multi-Hazard Early Warning System (MHEWS), in line with current climate challenges. Arthur said strengthening the National Climate Centre of Excellence is also a key focus to support research on climate change impacts and guide the development of more sustainable and resilient national adaptation policies.

items.title: Penang to launch first direct flight from Qingdao, boosting tourism and trade links
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 23 — Penang is set to achieve a significant milestone with the launch of its first direct flight connecting Qingdao, China and the state on March 31, marking a major step in enhancing air connectivity between Malaysia and China. State Tourism and Creative Economy Committee chairman Wong Hon Wai said that the new route, to be operated by Qingdao Airlines, represents a crucial development in strengthening bilateral ties, particularly in tourism, trade and cultural exchanges “With the introduction of this route, Qingdao will become the northernmost city in China currently connected to Penang via direct flights. “This important breakthrough reflects the steady northward expansion of Penang’s air connectivity with China, progressively enhancing our network coverage and strengthening overall connectivity,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Safety first, celebration second: Housing minister orders review after seventh fireworks stall fire destroys property
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming has ordered a review of standard operating procedures (SOPs) for temporary fireworks stalls following a fire in Nibong Tebal, Penang yesterday. Both the Fire and Rescue Department and the Local Government Department were directed to conduct the review, The New Straits Times reported today. According to The New Straits Times , the Taman Pekaka fire is the seventh fire involving fireworks stalls reported in Malaysia this year.

items.title: Anwar wishes Sultan Ibrahim health and blessings on official birthday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today conveyed his congratulations in conjunction with the official birthday of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia. “I humbly extend my highest congratulations and best wishes in conjunction with the official birthday of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, Sultan and Ruler of Johor Darul Ta’zim and the King of Malaysia, on March 23, 2026, corresponding to 3 Syawal 1447 Hijrah. “May Allah protect the Sultan,” he said.

items.title: Zahid thanks meteorologists for safeguarding public safety on World Meteorological Day
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi expressed his gratitude to all meteorologists nationwide for their commitment to ensuring public safety through continuous monitoring and the delivery of accurate weather information, in conjunction with World Meteorological Day 2026, observed today. “Today, I would like to express my deep appreciation to all meteorologists, especially the personnel of the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), for their commitment in continuously monitoring, analysing and delivering weather information to us,” he said in a Facebook post today. Themed ‘Observing Today, Protecting Tomorrow’, he said that the role of meteorology is becoming increasingly important in ensuring that every step taken today is able to protect the lives and well-being of the people in the future.

items.title: Sabah’s 40pc revenue claim negotiations cannot be rushed, says former Rela state honorary president
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 23 — Former honorary president of Persatuan Ahli-Ahli Kehormat Ikatan Relawan Rakyat Malaysia (Rela) Negeri Sabah, Datuk Jerry Ambuting Mohd Said, said Tuaran MP Datuk Wilfred Madius Tangau’s statement regarding the “countdown” for Sabah’s 40 per cent revenue claim does not reflect the full scope of the ongoing negotiation process. According to Jerry, negotiations based on the Malaysia Agreement 1963 are conducted through official channels, including the MA63 Consultative Council Technical Committee, which requires in-depth review and careful legal and fiscal consideration. “The negotiation process also takes into account various aspects, including financial implications to the country and the shared interests between the Federation and Sabah.

items.title: Police probe Nibong Tebal firecracker stall blaze, MBSP revokes permit
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 23 — The police have launched an investigation into a fire involving a firecracker and fireworks stall in Taman Pekaka, Nibong Tebal, yesterday. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said five reports have been lodged so far. Investigations are ongoing,” he said when contacted today.

items.title: Malaysia on track to meet VM2026 tourist target despite West Asia conflict, says Motac
items.summary: SEPANG, March 23 — The target for tourist arrivals in conjunction with Visit Malaysia 2026 (VM2026) remains on a positive track despite facing global uncertainties, particularly the conflict in West Asia, which has the potential to affect transit routes and the movement of international travellers. Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture’s (Motac) deputy secretary-general (Tourism) Chua Choon Hwa said the tourist arrival performance as of February showed encouraging progress, with the number of arrivals increasing by more than 30 per cent, compared with the same period last year, giving confidence that the VM2026 target remains realistic to achieve. As such, Chua said tourism strategies will be coordinated, including measures to address transit disruptions.

items.title: Durian trees bloom early in Penang as heatwave sparks hopes of longer, cheaper season
items.summary: BALIK PULAU, March 23 — Weeks of dry, hot weather have pushed durian trees in Penang into early bloom this year, and orchard owners say if conditions stay favourable, the harvest could stretch longer with more competitive prices by mid‑May into June. The rest will come from end‑April to mid‑August,” orchard owner Tan Chee Keat, 35, was quoted as saying. Tan added that the prolonged dry spell has helped flowering, but also required careful orchard management.

items.title: Malaysia to participate in WHO pandemic preparedness talks virtually amid West Asia conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Malaysia will participate in the 6th Intergovernmental Working Group (IGWG6) negotiation session at the World Health Organisation in Geneva, Switzerland, from today until Saturday, via an online platform due to the conflict in West Asia. The Ministry of Health (MOH), in a statement, said the participation involves collaboration with several relevant government agencies, including the Attorney General’s Chambers. MOH said Malaysia recognises the importance of the Pathogen Access and Benefit-Sharing System (PABS) negotiations in strengthening global pandemic preparedness.

items.title: Three-storey furniture shop in Bukit Bintang destroyed in fire, no casualties reported
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — A three-storey furniture shop in Bukit Bintang, here was destroyed in a fire last night. The Kuala Lumpur Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) operations centre said it received an emergency call on the incident at 10.58pm. “The fire affected the ground and second floors of the building, which housed furniture and perfume businesses, with damage estimated at 70 per cent,” JBPM said in a statement.

items.title: Hot and dry weather hits Padang Terap in Kedah at heatwave level as public urged to stay indoors
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 23 — Kedahans have been urged to limit outdoor activities and reduce exposure to direct sunlight following the hot and dry weather currently affecting most parts of the country. The water levels in rivers and dams in this state are also decreasing, so avoid wasting water. “There are areas facing water shortages, so do not waste water.

items.title: Health minister warns of 40°C danger as heatwave scorches Malaysia, claiming life of child left in car
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 23 — A total of 15 heat-related cases have been reported so far this year, including three cases of heatstroke and one death involving a child left in a vehicle, said Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad. The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has reported that hot weather conditions in Padang Terap, Kedah, have reached Level Two (heatwave), with daily maximum temperatures ranging between 37° and 40° Celsius. According to the statement, a heatwave level refers to daily maximum temperatures between 37 and 40°Celsius for at least three consecutive days, while the alert level refers to daily maximum temperatures between 35° and 37° Celsius for at least three consecutive days.

items.title: Smooth like butter: Klang Valley-bound traffic steady, highway authority warns of afternoon jams
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Traffic on major highways heading towards the Klang Valley is smooth and under control this morning. A spokesman for the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said the agency expects traffic to increase from noon today as people begin returning after celebrating Hari Raya Aidilfitri in their hometowns. According to him, PLUS has also activated smart lanes at 30 locations from 7 am to 11 pm today to help ease traffic flow.

items.title: Astro Awani editor’s 25-year-old son dies in Tanah Merah crash with JPJ; police clamp down on viral rumours
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 23 — Police are still conducting a comprehensive investigation into a crash involving a Road Transport Department (JPJ) vehicle and a car at KM85.5 of the Kota Bharu-Machang-Jeli road in Tanah Merah, on Friday. Tanah Merah District Police chief Supt Mohd Haki Hasbullah said the investigation covered various aspects, including witness statements, forensic analysis and technical examinations at the scene. Elaborating further, Mohd Haki said claims that police had made conclusions without basis were untrue.

items.title: Tragedy in Song: Missing teen found 100m from last seen point; searchers travel 65km to reach remote river site
items.summary: The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) said the victim, identified as Sylvester Jarbis Mesin, was reported to have gone out to bathe with two friends at about 12.30 pm before the incident. “The victim’s body was found by villagers at 3.15 pm, about 100 metres from where the victim was last seen, before being sent to the Nanga Bangkit Clinic,” said Sarawak JBPM in a statement today. Upon arrival at the location, the fire department collected information about the victim.

items.title: Search resumes for 66-year-old diver missing near Pulau Tenggol since Raya eve
items.summary: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) in a statement said so far no new leads have been reported by parties involved in the operation. “In addition, the operation was also assisted by the local diving community involving Tenggol Coral Beach Resort with 15 divers, Tenggol Dive Buddy Dungun (six divers) and Ocean Dream Dungun (six divers),” the statement said. The statement said the search operation was focused around the last location where the victim was reported missing and the nearby waters.

items.title: Family of six escapes as fire razes Kampung Gong Kijang home in Terengganu on second day of Raya
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, March 23 — A house occupied by a family of six was gutted by fire at Kampung Gong Kijang, near here, last evening. Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) operations commander Mohd Zaharim Yusof said three fire engines with 16 personnel from the Jalan Kota Fire and Rescue Station were deployed to the scene after an emergency call was received at 4.25 pm. Mohd Zaharim said no casualties were reported, and further investigations are ongoing.

items.title: DPM Fadillah: Malaysia can still afford RON95 subsidies for now, but RM2b monthly bill a concern
items.summary: KUCHING, March 23 — The federal government is closely monitoring global oil price movements amid concerns that crude prices could rise to US$100 per barrel, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Amar Fadillah Yusof. He said the government is currently able to sustain subsidies for RON95 petrol, although the subsidy burden remains substantial. However, Fadillah said the government is facing challenges due to fuel price differences with neighbouring countries.
